title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Epstein-Linked Model Recruiter Found Dead In France
stories.summary:
Epstein-Linked Model Recruiter Found Dead In France. A French model recruiter who had ties to the late convicted pedophile and sex trafficker Jeffrey Epstein was discovered dead in his home in France on Monday evening.

Daniel Siad, 69, was found dead in his home in Colombes — a suburb of Paris — Marie-Céline Lawrysz, the deputy public prosecutor at the Nanterre Judicial ... Al Jazeera: French model recruiter with close ties to Jeffrey Epstein found dead.

Al Jazeera reports that under investigation in France, Daniel Siad was accused of helping Epstein to traffic and abuse women.

stories.title: U.S., Saudi Arabia expected to sign nuclear deal this week

Paul, Minnesota, on Monday, taking the lives of three staff members following a disagreement over a garage parking contract.

stories.title: Indiana Jones Was Right: Zendaya’s Earrings Belong In A Museum
stories.summary:
Indiana Jones Was Right: Zendaya’s Earrings Belong In A Museum. When did valuable historical artifacts become red carpet accessories?

At the London photocall for the new Christopher Nolan adaptation of The Odyssey, actress Zendaya wore a pair of 3,000-year-old Iranian gold discs as earrings, intended as a nod to the film’s ancient setting and her role as the goddess Athena.

stories.title: Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI
stories.summary:
Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI. Software engineering was one of the best-paying professions in the US in 2022, but the advent of AI has disrupted it, leading to several layoffs and underemployment Every weekday, Matt, a software engineer, looks forward to his four-hour train commute to Pawling, New York.

It’s time he uses to work on his own project: a browser-based video game for which he writes every line of code himself. “I am actively trying to keep my axe sharp,” said Matt, who did not want to use his actual.

stories.title: Inside the horrifying online network where children coerce others to commit violence and self-harm
stories.summary:
Inside the horrifying online network where children coerce others to commit violence and self-harm. The 764 network is just one part of a growing online ecosystem that pushes young members into brutal and degrading acts.

It has perpetrators and victims in dozens of countries In late 2024, in a quiet suburb of Stockholm, a 14-year-old boy approached a man in his 80s who was using a walker.

He stabbed the man three times in the back. The video of the attack in Hässelby was streamed and spread quickly.

It fed a global online ecosystem that actively encourages and glorifies acts of.

stories.title: Bub – living an artist’s dreams and dreads in New York
stories.summary:
Bub – living an artist’s dreams and dreads in New York. An autobiographical game about the devastating effects of chronic illness on an artist’s creative life gives players the chance to slow the pace right down Bub does not look like a traditional video game.

Artist and stop-motion animator Case Jernigan spends hours, days, months in his studio drawing, painting and delicately cutting out pieces of collected paper, while coder and gameplay designer Todd Anderson builds scripts and physics simulations to make them into a game. <p.

stories.title: Leyte town builds Eastern Visayas’ 1st market wastewater treatment plant
stories.summary:
Leyte town builds Eastern Visayas’ 1st market wastewater treatment plant. TACLOBAN CITY — The local government unit of Burauen, Leyte has built the first-ever public market wastewater treatment facility in Eastern Visayas as part of its efforts to improve public sanitation and promote environmental sustainability.

Designed to collect, treat, and properly dispose of wastewater generated within the Burauen Commercial Complex, the P17-million facility has an estimated.

stories.title: Palace declares special non-working days in 11 localities
stories.summary:
Palace declares special non-working days in 11 localities. MANILA – Malacañang has declared special non-working days in 11 areas nationwide to allow residents’ full participation in local fiestas, founding anniversary celebrations, festivals and historical commemorations.

stories.title: Legarda calls for stronger action as child stunting rises
stories.summary:
Legarda calls for stronger action as child stunting rises.

MANILA, Philippines — No education reform can fully succeed while Filipino children continue to suffer from chronic malnutrition during their earliest years, Sen. Loren Legarda said in a statement issued on Tuesday as she called for stronger government action to fight child stunting.

Legarda called for stronger coordination among national government agencies, local government units, schools,.

stories.title: Government’s flagship one-stop shop launched in Maguindanao
stories.summary:
Government’s flagship one-stop shop launched in Maguindanao. MANILA, Philippines — President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Wednesday launched the Bangsamoro Convergence Program at Maguindanao del Norte.

The program gathers all government services in one stop to deliver frontline government assistance across the region.

The national government turned over P3.4 billion in projects and assistance to the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM).

stories.title: If you pay a hacker’s ransom, chances are that they’ll come back for more
stories.summary: If you pay a hacker’s ransom, chances are that they’ll come back for more. The long-held understanding among security researchers and network defenders is that it's impossible to negotiate in good faith with an extortion racket because there's no incentive for the other side to actually walk away.

stories.title: ‘We were at their mercy’: inside the Amazon tactics that hiked prices across the internet
stories.summary:
‘We were at their mercy’: inside the Amazon tactics that hiked prices across the internet. Internal emails revealed in court and interviews with ex-employees describe how Amazon’s practices drove up prices for competitors like Walmart and Target.

Amazon denies price-fixing claims and says it works to lower costs for consumers A “modern leather” table lamp, listed online at Walmart, shot up from $24.99 to $39. The price of an air fryer on a retail site called Newegg went from $84.99 to $149.99.

An electric ice-cream maker was listed at just $17.99 at Amazon and B.

stories.title: Splatoon Raiders review – Nintendo’s inky shooter fades to grey
stories.summary:
Splatoon Raiders review – Nintendo’s inky shooter fades to grey.

Nintendo Switch 2 The single-player offshoot of the quirky and colourful multiplayer series is disappointingly run-of-the-mill Splatoon is among Nintendo’s most ingenious inventions of the last decade: a series of shooting games whose paint-splattered turf war nature means being bad at shooting is just as valuable as – if not more so than – being good.

It is also blessed with an abundance of personality, with vibrant characters, a richly imagined world, and a hipness.

stories.title: The best LED face masks in the UK, tested: 12 light therapy devices that are worth the hype
stories.summary:
The best LED face masks in the UK, tested: 12 light therapy devices that are worth the hype. They claim to fix fine lines, blemishes, and redness – but which stand up to scrutiny?

After consulting dermatologists and putting them to the test, we have crowned a brand new number one • The best anti-ageing creams, serums and treatments LED face masks are booming in popularity – despite being one of the most expensive at-home beauty products to hit the market.
